Patch Set 3:

It will be restarted in appropriate ll_file_io_ptask().
So, main thread will restart only last chunk or whole IO if layout changed but after wait all previous submitted chaunks finished.

Patch Set 3:

This is how it looks in log file:
io write range: [1048576, 7340032) block - - io_parallel
submit write range: [1048576, 2097152)
submit write range: [2097152, 3145728)
submit write range: [3145728, 4194304)
loop write done: nob: 0, rc: -61 continue (in main thread)
done write range: [1048576, 2097152) ret: 1048576, rc: 0
done write range: [2097152, 3145728) ret: 1048576, rc: 0
done write range: [3145728, 4194304) ret: 1048576, rc: 0
return nob: 3145728, rc: -61 (in main thread)
losing layout lock
restart write range: [4194304, 7340032) ret: 4194304
io write range: [4194304, 7340032) block - - io_parallel
...

Patch Set 3:

> This is how it looks in log file:
 > io write range: [1048576, 7340032) block - - io_parallel
 > submit write range: [1048576, 2097152)
 > submit write range: [2097152, 3145728)
 > submit write range: [3145728, 4194304)
 > loop write done: nob: 0, rc: -61 continue (in main thread)
 > done write range: [1048576, 2097152) ret: 1048576, rc: 0
 > done write range: [2097152, 3145728) ret: 1048576, rc: 0
 > done write range: [3145728, 4194304) ret: 1048576, rc: 0
 > return nob: 3145728, rc: -61 (in main thread)
 > losing layout lock
 > restart write range: [4194304, 7340032) ret: 4194304
 > io write range: [4194304, 7340032) block - - io_parallel
 > ...

Your example only show the last chunk failure, which is able to handle by current implementation.

Try to return failure on writing [2097152, 3145728) you will know what I'm saying.

Patch Set 3:

Hmm. When I look into the code I see the following:
the IO is restarted only if ((rc == 0 || rc == -ENODATA) && count > 0 && io->ci_need_restart)

io->ci_need_restart set to 1 in vvp_io_fini and only when layout is chaged. So, we will restart IO only when layout is changed and there are no errors.

The example above I demonstrated the case when layout was changed in the middle of IO buffer. The buffer is from 1M to 7M but at the 4M the layout is changed with new PFL strategy. It works fine.

In case of real IO error the operation will not restart. It will return the error to user. Therefore we should not care about correct possition of Iter. So, I don't understad your concern. What exactly case you are talking about? Can you describe it in more details please or say how I can reproduce it?

Patch Set 3:

(1 comment)

Patch Set 3:

> Hmm. When I look into the code I see the following:
 > the IO is restarted only if ((rc == 0 || rc == -ENODATA) && count >
 > 0 && io->ci_need_restart)
 > 
 > io->ci_need_restart set to 1 in vvp_io_fini and only when layout is
 > chaged. So, we will restart IO only when layout is changed and
 > there are no errors.
 > 
 > The example above I demonstrated the case when layout was changed
 > in the middle of IO buffer. The buffer is from 1M to 7M but at the
 > 4M the layout is changed with new PFL strategy. It works fine.
 > 
 > In case of real IO error the operation will not restart. It will
 > return the error to user. Therefore we should not care about
 > correct possition of Iter. So, I don't understad your concern. What
 > exactly case you are talking about? Can you describe it in more
 > details please or say how I can reproduce it?

It may fail at writing [3M,4M), but writing to [0M,3M) and [5M,7M) succeed.

btw, please add an option so that users can disable parallel IO; this will also be beneficial for testing.

Patch Set 5: Code-Review+1

Please consider implementing a switch called "parallel_io" so that users can turn it off on the fly; this is also useful when to compare performance gains by parallel IO. Please take "fast_read" as an example.

You can choose to implement it in another patch.

parents.subject: LU-8964 libcfs: Introduce parallel tasks framework

commit.subject: LU-8964 clio: Parallelize generic I/O
commit.message:
LU-8964 clio: Parallelize generic I/O

Add parallel version of cl_io_loop() function which use information
about stripes from LOV layer and process them in parallel.
This feature is disabled by default. To enable it you should run
"lctl set_param llite.*.pio=1" command.

IOR results on KNL for:
access             = file-per-process
ordering in a file = sequential offsets
ordering inter file= no tasks offsets
clients            = 1 (1 per node)
repetitions        = 1
blocksize          = 128 GiB
aggregate filesize = 128 GiB

xfsize	pio	Write	Read
16	none	170.46	372.12
16	off	370.46	926.53
16	on	668.49	899.55
32	off	368.75	908.95
32	on	469.54	987.64

IOR results on Broadwell Xeon for:
access             = file-per-process
ordering in a file = sequential offsets
ordering inter file= no tasks offsets
clients            = 1 (1 per node)
repetitions        = 1
xfersize           = 16 MiB
blocksize          = 128 GiB
aggregate filesize = 128 GiB

pio	Write	Read
none	1419.80	1277.88
off	1348.98	2245.84
on	 990.76	2320.08

The scalability IOR results on other Broadwell Xeon for:
access             = file-per-process
ordering in a file = sequential offsets
ordering inter file= no tasks offsets
repetitions        = 1
xfersize           = 4 MiB
blocksize          = 8 GiB

Threads	pio	Write	Read
 32	off	9358.38	2649.28
 32	on	9147.14	2677.44
 64	off	8538.65	2811.05
 64	on	8944.19	2908.44
128	off	7978.61	2937.03
128	on	8613.91	2928.44

The numbers are in ‘MB/s’

Signed-off-by: Dmitry Eremin <dmitry.eremin@intel.com>
Change-Id: Id028faba1726fb377d0e903e8b8095d5ea9d1ee2
